title: coronilla minima var. lotoides
intro: Coronilla minima var. lotoides, also known as the small coronilla or lotoides coronilla, is a perennial plant belonging to the legume family and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Coronilla minima var. lotoides include its ability to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, decoction, or infusion for use in herbal remedies, with the dried flowers and leaves being used in various forms of traditional medicine.
